brief_document.title: Engine visibility integration and SDK search attribute binding
brief_document.purpose: Wire visibility into the engine: update the visibility index on every state-changing event, and provide the SDK binding for workflow authors to set custom search attributes from workflow code.
brief_document.task: Add a VisibilityStore to the EngineBuilder. After every state-changing event (WorkflowStarted, WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled, WorkflowContinuedAsNew, SearchAttributesUpdated), the engine upserts the visibility record. Add a Recorder method record_search_attributes_updated for the SDK to call. The SDK binding (workflow.upsert_search_attributes) records a SearchAttributesUpdated event which triggers the visibility update.

requirements.title: Engine updates visibility on state-changing events
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the VisibilityStore on WorkflowStarted, WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled, WorkflowContinuedAsNew, and SearchAttributesUpdated events. Each update upserts the full WorkflowSummary with current status, times, and search attributes.
acceptance: After WorkflowStarted, the workflow appears in list_workflows with status Running.
After WorkflowCompleted, the workflow's status is Completed and close_time is set.
After WorkflowFailed, the workflow's status is Failed.
After SearchAttributesUpdated, the workflow's search attributes are updated in the visibility index.

requirements.title: Recorder records SearchAttributesUpdated
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add record_search_attributes_updated to the Recorder that appends a SearchAttributesUpdated event with the provided attribute map. The Recorder SHALL validate attribute types against the registered schema.
acceptance: After recording, the event appears in read_history.
Setting an attribute with a type that doesn't match the schema returns an error.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement Gleam SDK bindings for upsert_search_attributes — those require the aion_flow crate and belong in a separate brief.
SHALL NOT implement server query endpoints — that is AV-005.
SHALL NOT implement NIF-level search attribute dispatch — the Recorder method is called from the host side.
verification: Tests cover: visibility update on each state-changing event, search attribute recording, type validation.

constraints.text: VisibilityStore is a trait in aion-store, not coupled to any specific backend.
The libSQL implementation must support filtering by all standard fields and custom attribute predicates without full table scans.
Search attribute types are registered per namespace; type mismatches are errors, not silent coercions.
Visibility updates must not block the EventStore append path in server mode.
WorkflowSummary must include enough data for dashboard rendering without per-workflow history reads.
The search attribute event (SearchAttributesUpdated) is a recorded event subject to replay and determinism — it must be replayable.

resolved_context.intention: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running workflows of type Y. Without a query layer, the only access path is by WorkflowId — which requires knowing the ID up front. This cluster adds search attributes (user-defined key-value metadata on workflows) and a visibility index that supports filtered listing, full-text-like search on attributes, and time-range queries. This is the operational backbone that makes dashboards, alerting, and debugging possible at scale.

provenance.quote: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running
